intent: Write a community-voices-meets-encyclopedia piece on 'why people in /f/books read the way they do'. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) Reddit — browse /f/books AND /f/writing for posts on reading habits, genre wars, dnf culture, or rereading. Collect at least 4 posts with title, score, comment count. (2) Wikipedia — open the articles on 'Reading (process)' and 'Genre fiction' (or closely related) and extract 3-4 factual anchors on how reading works and how genres are categorized. The report must: (a) summarize Wikipedia's framing of reading and genre, (b) surface the community's informal taxonomy of readers (speed-readers, rereaders, genre purists), (c) note where community practice diverges from or extends Wikipedia's formal categories, (d) close with one takeaway for each reader type. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Reddit (books + writing) + Wikipedia. Reader taxonomy synthesis, formal vs informal categories.
